sourceMovie.title: Swapped
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Swapped (2026) stuck because it commits to its animation premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Nathan Greno's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The animation and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Emperor's New Groove
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Swapped worked for you, The Emperor's New Groove (2000) is a strong next pick. The two films share animation and adventure throughlines and comic timing and sharper banter, even though the stories go in different directions.

faq.question: What should I watch after Swapped?
faq.answer: Try The Emperor's New Groove, Wonder Park, The Star—each shares DNA with Swapped while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Swapped?
faq.answer: Swapped (2026) is best described as Animation, Adventure, Comedy, Family, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Swapped worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
